subject: [PATCH] mm: mempolicy: fix automatic numa balancing for shmem

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Gregory Price expressed strong disagreement (CONTENTIOUS) with the patch, stating that demotions don't care about mempolicy and that opting shmem out of NUMA balancing on a tiered system is problematic. He also emphasized that this issue highlights the need for a complete overhaul of the demotion mechanism to prevent undefined behavior in memory placement.

Gave Reviewed-by
reviews.raw_body:
Eugh.

Demotions don't care about mempolicy, so opting shmem out of NUMA
balancing and mbind'ing on a tiered system is just full sadness.

This is all just more evidence that demotion needs to be completely
redone, it's creating a mess of undefined behavior for memory placement.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that the patch's handling of NULL from vm_ops->get_policy was incorrect, and agrees that using __get_vma_policy() directly is a better approach.
reviews.raw_body:
Right, mbinding in tiered mode is a whole other ball of wax. I'm just
trying to make the default case work ;-)

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch's change to use __get_vma_policy() directly creates a fallback to get_task_policy() when vma->vm_ops->get_policy returns NULL, which was not the case before and may cause confusion.
reviews.raw_body:
Okay, we used the fallback of vma->vm_policy before (if vma->vm_ops->get_policy
was not available), which is what __get_vma_policy() does at well.

But if vma->vm_ops->get_policy now returns NULL, we fallback to get_task_policy().


Makes sense to me although this is a source of confusion for me.

Acked-by: David Hildenbrand (Arm) <david@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,

David

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges the patch is addressing an old issue, but one that still affects real workloads on tiered systems, and agrees it's a straightforward bug that can be easily backported.
reviews.raw_body:
No objection from me. I was hesitant because it's old, and while these
are real workloads that see it they are hardware/kernel validation
runs. OTOH it's a straight-forward bug and should backport easily.

2026-06-29.patch_summary: This patch fixes a problem where mapped shared memory (shmem) was not considered for NUMA balancing on tiered systems. The issue arose from a previous commit that intended to limit NUMA scanning to migrate-on-fault VMAs, but incorrectly treated the absence of a shared policy as an explicit opt-out. The patch changes the vma_policy_mof function to use __get_vma_policy directly, allowing default policy VMAs to participate in balancing while still excluding explicitly pinned memory. This fix should improve performance on tiered systems with cachelib-based workloads.

reviews.author: Huang, Ying
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Huang noted that the patch does not handle the case where a vma's policy is NULL, and suggested adding a check for this condition to avoid potential crashes.

reviewer noted that the current implementation of mempolicy has issues during demotion and requested suggestions for improvement
reviews.raw_body:
Gregory Price <gourry@gourry.net> writes:

[snip]

---

It's hard to respect mempolicy during demotion in the current
implementation.  Do you have any ideas on how to improve this?

---
Best Regards,
Huang, Ying

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Reviewer Gregory Price suggested respecting per-vma mempolicies instead of per-task policies, which would make the interaction less painful and allow mbind() to behave as expected. He also noted that task-wide mempolicy is problematic on tiered systems and proposed documenting this limitation.
reviews.raw_body:
I think it's feasible we could respect per-vma mempolicies, but not
per-task.  That would at least make this particular interaction less
painful and mbind() would do what you'd expect.  It is a bit racy,
but with MPOL_MF_MOVE_ALL the user can get what they actually want.

I think task-wide mempolicy is problematic and generally a bad idea
on tiered systems, maybe it's ok if we simply document task policies
are not respected on tiered systems?

~Gregory

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch fixes a problem where mapped shared memory (shmem) was not considered for NUMA balancing on tiered systems. The issue arose from a previous commit that intended to limit NUMA scanning to migrate-on-fault VMAs, but incorrectly treated the absence of a shared policy as an explicit opt-out. The patch changes the vma_policy_mof function to use __get_vma_policy directly, allowing default policy VMAs to participate in balancing while still excluding explicitly pinned memory. This fix should improve performance on tiered systems with cachelib-based workloads.
